message:
feat: unify tombstone and closed status handling across codebase

This commit introduces consistent handling of the "tombstone" status as
semantically equivalent to "closed" throughout the bv codebase. Tombstone
issues are permanently archived issues that should be treated as completed
in all graph analysis, filtering, and display contexts.

Key Changes:

## Status Unification (isClosedLikeStatus helper)

Introduces the `isClosedLikeStatus(status model.Status) bool` helper function
in multiple packages (analysis/triage.go, export/markdown.go, ui/snapshot.go)
to provide a single predicate for "is this issue effectively closed". This
replaces direct `== model.StatusClosed` comparisons throughout the codebase.

Affected packages:
- pkg/analysis: priority.go, plan.go, diff.go, risk.go, whatif.go,
  dependency_suggest.go, label_suggest.go, label_health.go, duplicates.go,
  advanced_insights.go
- pkg/export: graph_export.go, graph_snapshot.go, markdown.go, mermaid_generator.go
- pkg/ui: model.go, board.go, graph.go, sprint_view.go, snapshot.go,
  background_worker.go
- pkg/correlation: related.go, file_index.go
- pkg/drift: drift.go
- pkg/search: normalizers.go

## Correlation Package Determinism

Adds `sortRelatedResults()` helper and `normalizeStatus()` to ensure
deterministic output ordering when finding related beads. Shared files
and commits are now pre-sorted before limiting to ensure reproducible
results across runs.

## Background Worker Metrics Enhancement

Extends WorkerMetrics with incremental vs full list rebuild tracking:
- IncrementalListCount: number of incremental updates
- FullListCount: number of full rebuilds
- IncrementalListRatio: efficiency metric for debugging

## Graph Stats Accessor Pattern (bv-77ec)

Updates priority.go to use the new CriticalPathValue() single-value
accessor instead of computing the full CriticalPathScore() map, reducing
memory allocations during what-if analysis.

## Dataset Tier System Foundation

Introduces datasetTier enumeration (small/medium/large/huge) based on
issue count thresholds. This enables adaptive behavior for large datasets
by configuring what computations to precompute during snapshot builds.

## Test Coverage

Comprehensive test updates across all affected packages ensure the new
tombstone handling works correctly in edge cases and maintains backward
compatibility with existing closed status behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -287,15 +287,15 @@ var (
 )
 
 func statusColor(s model.Status) color.RGBA {
-	switch s {
-	case model.StatusOpen:
+	switch {
+	case isClosedLikeStatus(s):
+		return colorClosed
+	case s == model.StatusOpen:
 		return colorOpen
-	case model.StatusBlocked:
+	case s == model.StatusBlocked:
 		return colorBlocked
-	case model.StatusInProgress:
+	case s == model.StatusInProgress:
 		return colorInProg
-	case model.StatusClosed:
-		return colorClosed
 	default:
 		return colorOpen
 	}

@@ -75,17 +75,19 @@ func GenerateMermaidGraph(issues []model.Issue, issueIDs map[string]bool, config
 
 		// Apply class based on status
 		var class string
-		switch i.Status {
-		case model.StatusOpen:
+		switch {
+		case isClosedLikeStatus(i.Status):
+			class = "closed"
+		case i.Status == model.StatusOpen:
 			class = "open"
-		case model.StatusInProgress:
+		case i.Status == model.StatusInProgress:
 			class = "inprogress"
-		case model.StatusBlocked:
+		case i.Status == model.StatusBlocked:
 			class = "blocked"
-		case model.StatusClosed:
-			class = "closed"
 		}
-		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("    class %s %s\n", safeID, class))
+		if class != "" {
+			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("    class %s %s\n", safeID, class))
+		}
 	}
 
 	sb.WriteString("\n")

@@ -264,15 +264,15 @@ func groupIssuesByMode(issues []model.Issue, mode SwimLaneMode) [4][]model.Issue
 		switch mode {
 		case SwimByStatus:
 			// Default: Open | In Progress | Blocked | Closed
-			switch issue.Status {
-			case model.StatusOpen:
+			switch {
+			case isClosedLikeStatus(issue.Status):
+				colIdx = 3
+			case issue.Status == model.StatusOpen:
 				colIdx = 0
-			case model.StatusInProgress:
+			case issue.Status == model.StatusInProgress:
 				colIdx = 1
-			case model.StatusBlocked:
+			case issue.Status == model.StatusBlocked:
 				colIdx = 2
-			case model.StatusClosed:
-				colIdx = 3
 			default:
 				colIdx = 0
 			}

@@ -875,30 +875,30 @@ func (g *GraphModel) renderMetricsPanel(id string, width int, t Theme) string {
 // Helper functions
 
 func getStatusIcon(status model.Status) string {
-	switch status {
-	case model.StatusOpen:
+	switch {
+	case isClosedLikeStatus(status):
+		return "✅"
+	case status == model.StatusOpen:
 		return "🔵"
-	case model.StatusInProgress:
+	case status == model.StatusInProgress:
 		return "🟡"
-	case model.StatusBlocked:
+	case status == model.StatusBlocked:
 		return "🔴"
-	case model.StatusClosed:
-		return "✅"
 	default:
 		return "⚪"
 	}
 }
 
 func getStatusColor(status model.Status, t Theme) lipgloss.AdaptiveColor {
-	switch status {
-	case model.StatusOpen:
+	switch {
+	case isClosedLikeStatus(status):
+		return t.Closed
+	case status == model.StatusOpen:
 		return t.Open
-	case model.StatusInProgress:
+	case status == model.StatusInProgress:
 		return t.InProgress
-	case model.StatusBlocked:
+	case status == model.StatusBlocked:
 		return t.Blocked
-	case model.StatusClosed:
-		return t.Closed
 	default:
 		return t.Secondary
 	}
